Professor Jonathan How mentions that future "smart" cars might use this system to help drivers avoid accidents. The algorithm was tested in Virginia and accurately identified potential violators a couple of seconds before reaching a red light. Also, compared to other models, it has fewer false alarms, which is crucial for systems guiding human drivers. The study is published in an IEEE journal.
